behavior_summary: U8P_CUSION::look is a simple examine/U8P_CUSION::look handler invoked when the player inspects the CUSION entity. It displays a single short text line: "cushion ".
After emitting the text it issues a suspend (pauses the process), which likely waits for the player to acknowledge/dismiss the displayed text.
There are no conditionals, menu interactions, global flag reads/writes, or external function calls in this script; behavior is a single display+pause sequence.
notable_control_flow: Linear, single-branch flow: show a bark/dialogue line then suspend the process.
No branching, no loops, and no calls to other in-project functions or engine APIs are present in the body.
open_questions: Does the 'suspend' token map to a player input wait (press key to continue) in the engine, or to a different process-scheduling behavior?
Is CUSION represented as an item instance or an NPC/entity in the game world (the script's content suggests a static object description but the type is not explicit)?
Is the trailing space in the text "cushion " intentional for concatenation/formatting, or an artifact of extraction?

main_function.name: U8P_CUSION::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short, on-screen description when the player examines the CUSION entity and then pause until dismissal.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the game's 'U8P_CUSION::look' (examine) action on the CUSION object/entity (process type 0x0208).

function_map.name: U8P_CUSION::look
function_map.role: Examine/U8P_CUSION::look handler that displays a short description and suspends; no parameters or side-effects on global state.
